content: THE FROST GIANTS
style_prompt: Talking with standard pitch, the man converses at a regular speed while exhibiting normal energy.

content: The Flatheads are Nannie's only neighbors, and perhaps you would consider them rather undesirable friends; but when I tell you how they came at once with blankets and food, and all sorts of friendly offers of shelter and help, you will think that some white people might well take a lesson from them.
style_prompt: With enthusiasm, the male speaker uses his low-pitched voice at a regular tempo, radiating energy.

content: They have been working all winter, but not quite so busily as now; for since yesterday they have cracked that big rock in two, and dug the great cave under the hill, and now they are gathered in council on the mountain side that overlooks a dashing little stream.
style_prompt: The boy's high-energy conversation is carried by his low-pitched voice and regular speaking speed.

content: WHAT THE FROST GIANTS DID TO NANNIE'S RUN
style_prompt: The male speaker's low pitch and dynamic speaking style convey high energy at a regular pace.

content: Can you imagine a beautiful oval shaped bay, almost encircled by a long arm of sand stretching out from the mainland?
style_prompt: The man conveys high energy through his low-pitched voice, speaking at a normal speed.

content: And, now, who is Nannie?
style_prompt: The man's high-energy speech is delivered swiftly, with his pitch remaining steady.

content: Three streams, straying from the far away mountains, and fed by their melted snows and hidden springs, find their way through the forest, leap and tumble over the cliff, and, passing through the little settlement, reach the sea.

content: Do you believe in giants?

content: THE INDIANS

content: You remember reading in your history, how, when our great great grandfathers came to this country to live, they found it occupied by Indians.

content: NANNIE'S RUN

content: Before many weeks have passed, some of the tall fir trees are cut down, and a new house is built, this time safely perched on top of the cliff; and, so far as I know, the Frost Giants have never succeeded in touching it.
style_prompt: The guy's dynamic voice bursts forth with high enthusiasm, while he maintains a regular pace of talking.

content: Oh, don't we wish the house had been built up on the cliff among the fir trees, safe above the reach of the water!
style_prompt: The man's low tone amplifies the excitement in his speech, delivered with a typical pace.

content: And this is what the Frost Giants did to Nannie's Run.

content: Her father and mother came here to live when she was but a baby, and before there was a single house built in the place; and it is out of compliment to her that one of the streams has been named Nannie's Run.
style_prompt: The male speaker conveys normal energy as he speaks rapidly with a low pitch.

content: Now that you are introduced, you will perhaps like to join a Frost party that started out to work, one day in the early spring of eighteen sixty one, from their homes among the Olympic Mountains.

content: Their deeds often seem to be cruel, and we cannot understand their meaning; but we shall some time know that the loving Father who sent them orders nothing for our hurt, but has always a loving purpose, though it may be hidden.

content: In its deep water the largest vessels might ride at anchor, but at the time of my story a lonelier place could scarcely be found.

content: Now and then they have to rake off a steep mountain side as you might a garden bed; and sometimes to bury a whole village so quickly that the poor inhabitants do not know what strange hand brought such sudden destruction upon them.

content: What will the stream do now?

content: It is indeed a very little settlement,--a few houses clustered together upon the sandy beach close to the blue water; behind the houses rises a cliff crowned with great fir trees, standing tall and dark in thick ranks, making a dense forest; and beyond this forest, cold, snow covered mountains lift their peaks against the sky,--a fitting home for the Frost Giants.
style_prompt: With a resonant voice, the male speaker exudes high energy as he speaks with a natural rhythm.

content: Now, if you will stay and watch it day after day, you will see what good result will come from this waiting; for every hour more and more water is running to its aid, and, as its forces increase, we begin to feel sure, that, although it can neither pass over nor under, it will some day be strong enough to break through the Frost Giants' dam.
style_prompt: The man's low pitch enhances his high-energy conversation, maintaining a natural tempo.

content: But here is Nannie hundreds and thousands of miles away from any such help; for there are not only no railroads to travel upon, but not even common roads nor horses nor wagons; nevertheless, there are neighbors who will bring help.
style_prompt: The man's low tone and natural speaking speed create an atmosphere of high energy while maintaining a natural pace.

content: We have not long to wait before we shall see, and hear too; for a great creaking and cracking begins, and, while we gaze astonished, the mountain side begins to slide, and presently, with a rush and a roar, dashes into the stream, and chokes it with a huge dam of earth and rocks and trees.
style_prompt: A male speaker's low-pitched voice conveys energy while speaking naturally at a moderate speed.

content: But this proves entirely unsuccessful; and at last, after whirling and tumbling, trying to creep under; trying to leap over, it settles itself quietly in its prison, as if to think about the matter.

content: The people who live here call these little streams RUNS, and one of them is Nannie's Run.
